text.title: Agentic Research Patterns
text.summary: Orchestrator pattern, prompt templates, CLI/SaaS reference table, and agent gotchas for AI-driven research pipelines.

section.title: Orchestrator Pattern

rule.statement: Deploy a Claude Sonnet orchestrator that forms queries, dispatches to tool-specific APIs, receives raw results, then passes to a synthesis step — never combining dispatch and synthesis in one call.
rule.rationale: Chaining dispatch and synthesis in a single call causes the agent to lose track of source attribution. Separate calls allow explicit source metadata to be passed through every step.

rule.statement: Set an iteration budget per stage (e.g., max 3 Perplexity queries for exploration) before the pipeline starts.
rule.rationale: Without a budget, agents spiral into diminishing-return queries, inflating cost and context. A fixed budget forces prioritization and prevents scope creep.

example.code: <research_task> <question>Top 5 AI-native research tools for market sizing in 2026?</question> <sources>perplexity, web-search</sources> <output_format>JSON: tool, use_case, pricing, agent_api_available</output_format> <verify>cross-reference at least 2 sources per tool claim</verify> </research_task>
example.note: Declares sources explicitly and requires cross-referencing. Prevents single-source hallucination.

example.code: <synthesis_task> <raw_findings>{{findings_json}}</raw_findings> <instruction>Identify contradictions. Flag any claim supported by fewer than 2 sources. Output structured markdown report.</instruction> </synthesis_task>
example.note: Synthesis step receives raw JSON from dispatch step. Source metadata travels through explicitly.

section.reference: XML prompt templates for research and synthesis tasks
Claude SDK dispatcher for multi-source synthesis

list.item: perplexity-python — Query Perplexity API from shell (pip install perplexity-python)
anthropic SDK — Claude synthesis driver (pip install anthropic)
serpapi — Structured Google/Bing results (pip install google-search-results)
Perplexity Pro — SaaS, REST API, best for cited multi-source synthesis
AlphaSense — SaaS, export-only, strong for SEC filings and earnings calls
Crayon — SaaS, webhook triggers, competitive intel; no natural-language query API
Brandwatch — SaaS, REST API, social sentiment; complex auth setup
Statista — SaaS, download only; agent must parse PDFs
Google Trends — free, pytrends library, rate-limited, trend signals only
Dovetail — SaaS, CSV export, requires human-uploaded transcripts

antipattern.description: Perplexity API results differ from the web UI (no "Pro Search" toggle in API v1; use search_recency_filter instead).
antipattern.reason: Testing only the web UI and then relying on the API will produce coverage gaps; both must be validated before committing to API-only flow.

antipattern.description: Synthesis agents lose track of source attribution when chaining multiple tool calls without explicit metadata passthrough.
antipattern.reason: Source metadata must be passed explicitly through every pipeline step, or the final report cannot be audited for citation quality.

antipattern.description: Free-tier rate limits (Google Trends, SerpAPI) silently truncate results without error.
antipattern.reason: Truncated results look complete but are not; add explicit error handling and result-count validation for every rate-limited tool.
